22.name: Directorate-General for Cohesion, Agriculture and Social Policies
22.refined_description: The Directorate-General for Cohesion, Agriculture and Social Policies (Unit 22) operates as a cornerstone strategic coordination body within the European Parliament's organizational framework, serving to strengthen Parliament's legislative, budgetary, and control functions across critical EU policy domains while defining institutional strategy and ensuring comprehensive development of general management functions. The directorate-general allocates equal priority (25% each) to strategic leadership through the Parliamentary Reform 2024 program implementation and expert committee support, and to administrative coordination of parliamentary committees covering cohesion policies, agriculture, fisheries, transport, tourism, social policies, and the EU housing crisis. Critical institutional relationships are maintained through representation of general management within Parliament bodies, monitoring parliamentary work consistency, and fostering cooperation with key internal services including DG ECTI, IUST, BUDG, EXPO, the Directorate for Coordination of Committees and Legislative Activities (CCLA), DG PRES, and Cabinet services (15% allocation). The unit manages comprehensive human resources and budgetary responsibilities in collaboration with management teams (15% allocation), while representing general management in interinstitutional relations (10% allocation). Operational coordination encompasses central secretariat functions, procedural compliance with internal rules (5% allocation), and delegated authorizing officer responsibilities (5% allocation). The directorate-general provides direct oversight of three specialized organizational units: the Directorate for Transport, Employment and Social Affairs (22B), the Directorate for Regional Development, Agriculture and Fisheries (22A), and the Resources Unit (22-10).
22.refined_activity_summary:
• Strategic Leadership & Parliamentary Reform Implementation (25%): Define institutional strategy, develop general management functions, strengthen Parliament's legislative/budgetary/control powers through Parliamentary Reform 2024 program execution and expert committee support
• Committee Administration & Policy Coordination (25%): Ensure administrative coordination and proper functioning of parliamentary committees across cohesion policies, agriculture, fisheries, transport, tourism, social policies, and EU housing crisis
• Institutional Coordination & Internal Collaboration (15%): Represent general management in Parliament bodies, monitor parliamentary work consistency, and cooperate with internal services including DG ECTI, IUST, BUDG, EXPO, CCLA, DG PRES, and Cabinet services
• Human Resources & Budget Management (15%): Manage comprehensive human resources and general management budget administration in partnership with management teams
• Interinstitutional Relations & External Representation (10%): Represent general management in interinstitutional relations and external coordination activities
• Central Secretariat Coordination & Compliance (5%): Coordinate general management procedures, central secretariat operations, and ensure compliance with internal parliamentary rules
• Delegated Authorizing Officer Functions (5%): Execute delegated financial authorization responsibilities and related oversight duties

22-10.name: Resources Unit
22-10.refined_description: The Resources Unit (22-10) functions as the comprehensive administrative and strategic backbone for the Directorate-General for Cohesion, Agriculture and Social Policies within the European Parliament, orchestrating essential support functions that enable the DG's policy-focused mission across cohesion, agriculture, and social policy domains. The unit's core operational foundation rests on integrated human resources management and financial administration, which together constitute 40% of its workload through complete staff recruitment processes, organizational chart management, performance evaluations and rating exercises, staff rights and obligations administration, mobility exercises (20%), alongside centralized financial system operations through FMS and Webcontracts IT systems, budget verification procedures, and comprehensive financial oversight (20%). Strategic planning and governance activities account for 15% of operations, encompassing strategic planning cycles, risk management frameworks, business continuity planning, EMAS environmental management actions, and coordination of simplification initiatives that enhance organizational effectiveness. The unit dedicates significant capacity to operational support services totaling 28%, including training coordination with DG PERS for internal training programs, external training organization, study visits, secondments, and career advisory services (10%), comprehensive logistics and infrastructure management with systematic liaison to DG ITEC, DG CASP, and DG INLO for IT coordination and space management (10%), and specialized mission budget management including overtime administration (8%). Additionally, the unit manages critical budgetary coordination and internal control functions (10%) encompassing annual budget drafting, monitoring, transfers, audit coordination, and activity reporting, while providing essential internal communication services including website management, event organization, and visual identity compliance (7%). This integrated approach ensures seamless operational coordination across all DG units while maintaining the administrative excellence required for effective policy implementation within the broader European Parliament institutional framework.
22-10.refined_activity_summary:
• Human Resources & Staff Management (20%): Complete recruitment management, organizational chart oversight, staff rights and obligations administration, performance evaluations, rating exercises, and mobility exercise coordination
• Financial Management & Systems (20%): Centralized financial system management through FMS and Webcontracts, budget verification procedures, financial oversight, and comprehensive financial administration
• Strategic Planning & Governance (15%): Strategic planning cycles, risk management frameworks, business continuity planning, EMAS environmental management coordination, and organizational simplification initiatives
• Budget Coordination & Internal Control (10%): Annual budget drafting and monitoring, budget transfers, internal control systems, audit coordination, and comprehensive activity reporting
• Training & Professional Development (10%): Internal training coordination with DG PERS, external training organization, study visits, secondments, and career advisory services
• Logistics & Infrastructure Support (10%): Space management, IT coordination with DG ITEC/DG CASP/DG INLO, infrastructure liaison, and comprehensive facility management
• Mission Budget & Overtime Management (8%): Specialized mission budget administration, overtime management, and related financial coordination
• Internal Communication & Events (7%): Website management, internal communication coordination, event organization, and visual identity compliance

22A.name: Directorate for Regional Development, Agriculture and Fisheries
22A.refined_description: The Directorate for Regional Development, Agriculture and Fisheries (22A) serves as the strategic leadership and coordination hub within the Directorate-General for Cohesion, Agriculture and Social Policies, orchestrating comprehensive parliamentary support across regional development, agricultural, and fisheries policy domains through four specialized child units—the secretariats of the Committees on Fisheries, Agriculture and Rural Development, and Regional Development, plus a dedicated Policy Department. The directorate allocates its primary operational capacity (60%) to ensuring strategic direction, coordination, and animation of its constituent units and services, while managing personnel resources and overseeing the general organizational framework of the directorate. Parliamentary advisory functions constitute 20% of the directorate's workload, encompassing direct advisory support to Members of the European Parliament in specialized areas (10%), including essential assistance to parliamentary management committee members, particularly supporting presidents and rapporteurs in executing their institutional functions, and provision of high-level expertise, contemporary analysis, and independent research to parliamentary committees (10%) within the directorate's specialized domains. The directorate maintains critical financial oversight responsibilities through its subdelegated authorizing officer function (10%), ensuring fiscal accountability and administrative compliance within its operational mandate. Strategic coordination activities (10%) encompass project management coordination across specialized management units and services (5%) alongside institutional representation in internal committees and interinstitutional forums (5%), maintaining strategic partnerships across the European institutional landscape and ensuring seamless coordination with Parliament's internal services.
22A.refined_activity_summary:
• Leadership and Organizational Management (60%): Direction, coordination and animation of constituent units and services, personnel management, and general organizational framework oversight
• Parliamentary Advisory Services (20%): Direct advisory support to MEPs in specialized areas including assistance to parliamentary management committee members, presidents and rapporteurs (10%), and provision of high-level expertise, contemporary analysis, and independent research to parliamentary committees (10%)
• Financial and Administrative Oversight (10%): Exercise of subdelegated authorizing officer functions for fiscal accountability and administrative compliance
• Strategic Coordination and Representation (10%): Project management coordination across specialized management units and services (5%) and institutional representation in internal committees and interinstitutional forums with inter-service liaison and strategic partnership maintenance (5%)

22A10.name: Policy Department
22A10.refined_description: The Policy Department (22A10) operates as a specialized research and analytical unit within the Directorate for Regional Development, Agriculture and Fisheries, serving as the primary source of comprehensive policy expertise and research support to parliamentary bodies and committee secretariats. The department's core mission centers on delivering high-quality studies, notes, and research across regional development, agriculture and rural development, and fisheries policies (35% allocation), providing direct analytical support to the REGI, AGRI, and PECH committees within the European Parliament's legislative framework. The unit maintains rigorous academic and scientific standards through comprehensive external expertise management, encompassing both the procurement of specialized knowledge via tender procedures and contract negotiations (25% allocation) and the systematic quality control oversight of all externally-developed studies to ensure scientific rigor (20% allocation). Strategic partnership development represents a critical operational component (10% allocation), involving active coordination and relationship maintenance with DG EPRS services, external experts, research institutes, universities, and internal Parliamentary services to enhance the department's research capacity and institutional knowledge network. Additionally, the department ensures effective knowledge dissemination and continuous improvement through the production and regular updating of thematic documentation sheets (5% allocation) and systematic monitoring and promotion of the impact of its expertise provision (5% allocation). Through this comprehensive, multi-faceted approach, the Policy Department ensures that parliamentary decision-making across its specialized policy domains is consistently supported by rigorous, scientifically-sound research and analysis that meets the highest academic standards while remaining directly relevant to legislative needs.
22A10.refined_activity_summary:
• Core Research & Policy Analysis Support (35%): Direct provision of studies, notes, and research assistance to parliamentary bodies and committee secretariats, specifically supporting REGI, AGRI, and PECH committees across regional development, agriculture and rural development, and fisheries policy domains
• External Expertise Procurement & Management (25%): Complete management of tender procedures and contract negotiations for securing specialized external research expertise and consultant services
• Scientific Quality Control & Assurance (20%): Systematic oversight and quality control of all externally-developed studies to ensure scientific rigor and academic standards compliance
• Strategic Partnership Development & Coordination (10%): Active relationship maintenance and coordination with DG EPRS services, external experts, research institutes, universities, and internal Parliamentary services to enhance research capacity and institutional knowledge networks
• Knowledge Management & Documentation (5%): Production, writing, and regular updating of thematic sheets and specialized policy documentation
• Impact Assessment & Promotion (5%): Systematic monitoring, evaluation, and promotion of the impact and utilization of expertise provided to enhance departmental effectiveness and visibility

22A20.name: Secretariat of the Committee on Regional Development
22A20.refined_description: The Secretariat of the Committee on Regional Development (22A20) operates as the specialized administrative and policy support hub within the Directorate for Regional Development, Agriculture and Fisheries, serving as the primary interface between the European Parliament's Committee on Regional Development and its Members. The secretariat's core mission centers on providing comprehensive legislative and administrative assistance to MEPs, dedicating 55% of its resources to delivering written and oral support to Members serving as presidents, vice-presidents, rapporteurs, and opinion drafters, including the preparation of critical working documents, draft reports for both legislative and non-legislative procedures, and draft opinions. Administrative coordination constitutes 25% of the workload through managing essential committee secretariat functions including commission meetings, coordinators' meetings, working group establishment and monitoring, programming activities, trilogue coordination, and comprehensive minute-taking and documentation services. The unit allocates 10% of its activities to external engagement initiatives, encompassing the organization of public hearings and external commission missions outside the workplace, alongside active participation in committee monitoring activities. The remaining 10% focuses on specialized stakeholder relations and documentation oversight, maintaining institutional relationships with other EU institutions, regional organizations, and civil society groups while coordinating with Parliament's internal services and systematically monitoring regional policy programming documents.
22A20.refined_activity_summary:
• Legislative and Policy Support (55%): Direct written and oral assistance to MEPs in leadership roles as presidents, vice-presidents, rapporteurs, and opinion drafters, including preparation of working documents, draft reports for legislative and non-legislative procedures, and draft opinions
• Administrative Coordination (25%): Comprehensive committee secretariat operations including commission meeting management, coordinators' meetings, working group establishment and monitoring, programming activities, trilogue coordination, and minutes preparation and documentation
• External Engagement and Outreach (10%): Organization of public hearings and external commission missions outside the workplace, participation in committee monitoring activities, and stakeholder relations with EU institutions, regional organizations, and civil society groups
• Specialized Documentation and Institutional Relations (10%): Systematic monitoring of regional policy programming documents and coordination with Parliament's internal services to ensure seamless institutional operations

22B0040.name: Secretariat of the Special Committee on the Housing Crisis in the European Union
22B0040.refined_description: The Secretariat of the Special Committee on the Housing Crisis in the European Union (Unit 22B0040) operates as a specialized administrative and operational support unit within the European Parliament's Directorate for Transport, Employment and Social Affairs, serving as the dedicated institutional backbone for Parliament's investigative body addressing Europe's critical housing affordability challenges. 22B20.name: Secretariat of the Committee on Transport and Tourism

22B30.name: Secretariat of the Committee on Employment and Social Affairs

ORG_MASTER.name: European Parliament Organizations
ORG_MASTER.refined_description: The European Parliament Organizations unit (ORG_MASTER) functions as the foundational apex organizational framework within the European Parliament's institutional hierarchy, operating at the root level (-1) directly under the Secretary General's authority as the comprehensive master registry for all directorate-general structures and administrative units. This meta-organizational entity serves as both the primary structural container and institutional umbrella that encompasses, coordinates, and provides oversight for all specialized directorates-general operations within the Parliament's administrative architecture, including units such as the Directorate-General for Cohesion, Agriculture and Social Policies. While the master unit itself executes no direct operational activities, it establishes the essential organizational infrastructure that enables systematic management, ensures coordinated governance, and facilitates effective institutional coherence across the Parliament's diverse organizational components. The unit's strategic positioning as the institutional backbone underscores its fundamental role in maintaining operational continuity, supporting hierarchical integration, and serving as the primary organizational reference point that enables coherent governance structures between the Secretary General's office and all subordinate specialized units. This organizational framework ensures strategic alignment with the Parliament's legislative and representative mandates while providing the foundational architecture necessary for the systematic coordination of all directorate-general functions within the European Parliament's comprehensive institutional structure.
ORG_MASTER.refined_activity_summary:
• Institutional Framework Management & Master Registry Operations (0%): No direct operational activities assigned - serves as comprehensive organizational foundation and master registry for all directorate-general structures
• Administrative Coordination & Governance Oversight (0%): Provides institutional architecture, coordinated governance, and systematic oversight infrastructure for specialized directorate-general operations
• Strategic Hierarchical Integration & Structural Support (0%): Maintains root-level positioning, ensures institutional coherence, and facilitates strategic alignment across all Parliament units under Secretary General authority
